motivation: N2-A's qodec-n2-miner-canary.yml requested dotnet 8.0.x via actions/setup-dotnet, but the confined build actually executed under /usr/share/dotnet/sdk/10.0.301 — nothing compared the requested range against what was resolved and what was executed, so the divergence went unnoticed until direct log inspection. This contract makes that comparison mandatory and structural.

requested.description: The version or range asked for, before any resolution happens.
example_sources: a workflow's setup-* action version input

resolved.description: What the toolchain resolver actually picked, captured as trusted setup evidence (dotnet --info / rustc --version / python --version / mvn --version / gradle --version), before untrusted execution begins.

executed.description: What the confined build process actually invoked and ran as argv[0] — must be the exact same absolute path used to capture the 'resolved' evidence, so identity can never silently drift across a sudo/privilege boundary.

classifications.compatible-resolution: resolved_version satisfies a floating requested range (e.g. 8.0.x) under a documented resolution policy.
classifications.unexpected-resolution: resolved_version neither matches nor satisfies the requested range — this is exactly the N2-A finding (8.0.x requested, 10.0.301 executed).
classifications.identity-missing: one or more required resolved/executed fields is null or empty — ALWAYS a hard failure; a setup action's input string is a request, never evidence of what actually ran.

non_empty_equality_rule: Two receipts both carrying a null or empty value for the same identity field must never be reported as 'in agreement' — see receipt_contract.py's REQUIRE_NON_EMPTY_PATHS and compare_receipts().
